title: Auxiliary censored time-to-next-pit survival model
summary: This task is a one-lap hazard slice of a broader time-to-next-stop problem.
rationale: This task is a one-lap hazard slice of a broader time-to-next-stop problem. Survival literature and XGBoost’s AFT objective are designed for dynamic event timing with censoring, which fits rows near race end that may never pit again.
implementation_hint: Derive fold-local labels for laps-until-next-pit within each driver-race sequence, right-censor at race finish when no further pit occurs, and fit an auxiliary survival model (`survival:aft` or discrete-time landmarked hazard). Feed predicted 1-lap event probability, 2-3 lap survival mass, or residuals into the main ROC-AUC model.
expected_effect: Sharper probability ordering around ambiguous medium-horizon windows where next-lap labels alone are noisy.
risk: Target construction is easy to leak if future laps from validation races inform encoders or calibrators; all auxiliary predictions must be strict OOF.
